tweet: This is the history and context to know when Jordan is described as growing up “near U.S. Highway 117,” and when you hear Bulls announcer Ray Clay famously introduce MJ as “And now, from North Carolina...”

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: “Keeping your head down & your thoughts to yourself, working hard, never trusting, never easing up even for a moment. It was a choice. Michael Jordan was born into a world of predators, & into a line of survivors, & he studied how to win. That's the real wonder of him up close.”

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: “Three days later, white preachers of Wilmington told their congregation they'd done the Lord's work.” In 1889, the town was 56% black. Today 18%. For MJ’s first 14 years, he knew and heard this history from his great-grandfather who survived this reign of terror.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: Wilmington on the eve of the 20th century was a model American city. 3 of 10 aldermen were black. Ten of the 26 policemen. Then a racist militia in broad daylight killed at least 60 black people, forced the mayor and all aldrerman to resign, burned down the black newspaper.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: “His people hunt deer, fish for catfish and bream, raise hogs and chickens and regularly attend church. Jordan grew up with a military father and a New Testament mother, both of whom grew up in Old Testament homes. Hard work as the only portal from that plane of existence.”

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: “His family spent at least six generations in one small patch of swamp and cropland in the rural outskirts and farm towns near Wilmington, on and around Highway 117. He remembers his grandparents still eating dirt and clay -- a now little getting needed iron from the land.”

name: Tom Perriello

tweet: 12. Eliminate human trafficking in Virginia. Last year 148 human trafficking cases were reported in VA — average of nearly 1 every other day

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 11. Keep guns from hands of stalkers & abusers. When a domestic abuser has a gun, the relationship is 500% more likely to end in homicide.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 10. Expand Medicaid coverage. This would give hundreds of thousands of Virginia women the care and access they deserve.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 9. Veto legislative attacks on women’s health. Since '10, Rs in VA have proposed 75+ restrictions on women’s reproductive health care.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 8. Increase funding for women’s health centers and family planning services. Access to health care shouldn't depend on your ZIP code.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 7.Ensure choice w/ dignity by ending the 24hr waiting period, biased counseling & mandatory ultrasounds for women seeking abortion services.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 6. Combat rape and sexual assault. In Virginia there are more than 5,000 rapes & sexual assaults each year. This is completely unacceptable.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 5.Eliminate the ‘Tampon Tax’ in Virginia. We exempt hearing aids &eyeglasses from sales tax but not feminine hygiene products. That's wrong.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 4. End the gender pay gap. This gap in income and wealth is even more pronounced among African-American women and Latinas in Virginia.

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 3. Help Virginians afford child care. Too many Virginia families are forced to spend more on childcare than on housing .

name: Tom Perriello
tweet: 2. Push family leave and guaranteed medical leave to care for new children, aging parents, or an ill family member. This should be a given.
